interfaces.name: TitleType
interfaces.signature: enum TitleType: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case movie, tv }
interfaces.notes: Track boundary is by title type; movie is free ranked track, TV ranked only with entitlement.

interfaces.name: RankingState
interfaces.signature: enum RankingState: String, Codable, Sendable { case unranked, insertionInProgress, ranked }
interfaces.notes: Uncontested across both rounds.

interfaces.name: Genre
interfaces.signature: enum Genre: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case action, comedy, drama, horror, thriller, sciFi, fantasy, animation, documentary, romance, other }
interfaces.notes: Fixed single-pick enum, never free text.

interfaces.name: FilmTitle
interfaces.signature: struct FilmTitle: Identifiable, Equatable, Hashable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var name: String; var type: TitleType; var year: Int?; var genre: Genre?; var posterSeed: String; var rankingState: RankingState; var trackRankPosition: Int?; var createdAt: Date; var yearDecade: Int? { ... } }
interfaces.notes: Domain projection used across layers; keep separate from SwiftData persistence model.

interfaces.name: StoredTitle
interfaces.signature: struct StoredTitle: Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var name: String; var typeRaw: String; var year: Int?; var genreRaw: String?; var posterSeed: String; var rankingStateRaw: String; var position: Int; var insertedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: SwiftData entity shape serialized through repository; defaults on all non-optional fields.

interfaces.name: ComparisonRecord
interfaces.signature: struct ComparisonRecord: Identifiable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let flowID: UUID; let winnerID: UUID; let loserID: UUID; let track: TitleType; let wasSkipped: Bool; let timestamp: Date }
interfaces.notes: Append-only comparison trail for auditability and future analytics-free troubleshooting.

interfaces.name: InsertionContext
interfaces.signature: @Model final class InsertionContext { var id: UUID; var titleID: UUID; var flowID: UUID; var mediaType: MediaType; var lo: Int; var hi: Int; var lastComparedTitleID: UUID?; var skipBudget: Int; var createdAt: Date; var updatedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: FINAL: lastComparedTitleID is UUID?, not an index — both sides agreed in round 2. Single source of truth for crash-recoverable active insertion.

interfaces.name: TitleRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol TitleRepository { func fetchAll(track: MediaType?) async throws -> [Title]; func fetchRanked(track: MediaType) async throws -> [Title]; func title(id: UUID) async throws -> Title?; func add(name: String, mediaType: MediaType, year: Int?, genre: Genre?) async throws -> Title; func delete(_ id: UUID) async throws; func applyPositions(_ updates: [(id: UUID, position: Int)], track: MediaType) async throws; func capReached(track: MediaType) async throws -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: applyPositions is one atomic write per insertion, bulk shift unanimated. capReached folded in from Codex round 2 for the 25-item movie cap check.

interfaces.name: TitleFilter
interfaces.signature: struct TitleFilter: Equatable, Sendable { var genre: Genre?; var decade: Int? }
interfaces.notes: Decade derived from year at read time, never stored as free text.

interfaces.name: RankingEngine
interfaces.signature: enum RankingEngine { static func startInsertion(newID: UUID, rankedIDs: [UUID]) -> InsertionState; static func nextPair(state: InsertionState, rankedIDs: [UUID]) -> ComparisonPair?; static func applyChoice(state: InsertionState, chosenID: UUID, rankedIDs: [UUID]) -> InsertionStepResult; static func applySkip(state: InsertionState, rankedIDs: [UUID]) -> InsertionStepResult }
interfaces.notes: FINAL: static-func enum, both sides agreed. Lives in the standalone FaveOffRanking local package (Foundation-only) so a SwiftData/SwiftUI import here is a compile error.

interfaces.name: RankingStep
interfaces.signature: struct RankingStep: Sendable { let context: InsertionContext; let leftID: UUID; let rightID: UUID; let isTerminal: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Defines compare pair surfaced to UI when not terminal.

interfaces.name: RankingTransition
interfaces.signature: struct RankingTransition: Sendable { let updatedContext: InsertionContext?; let placedID: UUID?; let newPosition: Int?; let fallbackReason: SkipFallbackReason?; let neighbors: (UUID?, UUID?) }
interfaces.notes: Returned after each step; terminal transitions include placement payload and neighbors.

interfaces.name: SkipFallbackReason
interfaces.signature: enum SkipFallbackReason: String, Sendable { case twoSkips; case userCancelled; case deletedOpponent }
interfaces.notes: Explicit transparency for copy and analytics-safe behavior.

interfaces.name: MetadataProvider
interfaces.signature: protocol MetadataProvider { func enrich(title: FilmTitle) async -> PosterMetadata; }
interfaces.notes: Default implementation must be local/offline stub; remote provider optional and additive.

interfaces.name: PosterMetadata
interfaces.signature: struct PosterMetadata: Sendable { let resolvedName: String; let year: Int?; let genre: Genre?; let posterImageData: Data?; let posterColorHex: String; let textSeed: String }
interfaces.notes: Keep fallback deterministic from local stub.

interfaces.name: PurchaseService
interfaces.signature: protocol PurchaseService { var state: PurchaseState { get }; func loadProducts() async throws -> [PurchaseProduct]; func purchase(_ productID: ProductID) async throws; func restore() async throws; func isEntitled(to feature: PremiumFeature) -> Bool; func startEntitlementListener() async; func stopEntitlementListener() }
interfaces.notes: Both sides converged on this exact merged shape in round 2. startEntitlementListener runs Transaction.currentEntitlements as a persistent launch-time Task.

interfaces.name: PurchaseResult
interfaces.signature: enum PurchaseResult: Sendable { case purchased; case pending; case cancelled; case failed(Error) }
interfaces.notes: Sheet state machine should map from this to non-blocking UI transitions.

interfaces.name: TasteCardRenderer
interfaces.signature: @MainActor struct TasteCardRenderer { func renderCard(titles: [Title]) async throws -> Image }
interfaces.notes: Returns Image, matching TasteCardRenderState.ready(Image) directly. Only fires once every source poster image is confirmed loaded — no ImageRenderer snapshot race.

interfaces.name: LibraryViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class LibraryViewModel { var titles: [Title]; var selectedTrack: MediaType; var activeFilters: FilterState; var pendingResume: ActiveInsertionFlow?; var isAtCap: Bool; func load() async; func addTitle(name: String, mediaType: MediaType, year: Int?, genre: Genre?) async throws -> ActiveInsertionFlow?; func removeTitle(_ id: UUID) async throws; func resumeFlow() async throws -> ActiveInsertionFlow? }
interfaces.notes: @Observable (not ObservableObject) for property-level invalidation. addTitle/resumeFlow both produce the identical ActiveInsertionFlow shape, matching the single-cover invariant.

interfaces.name: CompareFlowViewModel
interfaces.signature: final class CompareFlowViewModel: ObservableObject { @MainActor func loadNextPair() async; @MainActor func choose(_ id: UUID) async; @MainActor func skip() async; @MainActor func cancelBecauseDeletedOpponent() }
interfaces.notes: Single-screen ritual inside one full-screen cover; fetches both titles by persistent ID before each action.

interfaces.name: PaywallViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class PaywallViewModel { var state: PurchaseState; var products: [PurchaseProduct]; var openFrom: PaywallTrigger; func loadProducts() async; func purchase(_ id: ProductID) async; func restore() async }
interfaces.notes: Dismissal handled by @Environment(\.dismiss) at the view layer, not a view-model method — the sheet owns its own presentation lifecycle. openFrom folded in from Codex round 2 to drive contextual copy.

interfaces.name: PrivacyManifestSpec
interfaces.signature: func buildPrivacyManifestEntry() -> [String: Any]
interfaces.notes: Adopted from Codex's round-1 addition as-is — CI-checked helper keeping xcprivacy fields explicit and stable.

interfaces.name: Title
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Title { var id: UUID; var name: String; var mediaType: MediaType; var year: Int?; var genre: Genre?; var posterColorSeed: String; var posterInitials: String; var rankingState: RankingState; var position: Int?; var createdAt: Date; var comparisonCount: Int }
interfaces.notes: FINAL: no separate domain/persistence DTO layer; used directly through TitleRepository. No @Relationship, no @Attribute(.unique) on id — deliberately dropped from Codex's round-2 draft since unique attributes break CloudKit-sync compatibility per ios_architecture_review; UUID() generation already gives practical uniqueness. Reference type despite kind=struct (schema has no 'class' option).

interfaces.name: Comparison
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Comparison { var id: UUID; var winnerID: UUID; var loserID: UUID; var mediaType: MediaType; var timestamp: Date; var wasSkipped: Bool; var flowID: UUID }
interfaces.notes: Append-only audit trail; stays valid after the referenced Title is deleted.

interfaces.name: MediaType
interfaces.signature: enum MediaType: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case movie, tv }
interfaces.notes: Track discriminator; movie is free-ranked, tv is addable but ranking-gated until entitled.

interfaces.name: FaveOffSchemaV1
interfaces.signature: enum FaveOffSchemaV1: VersionedSchema { static var models: [any PersistentModel.Type] { [Title.self, Comparison.self, InsertionContext.self] }; static var versionIdentifier: Schema.Version { Schema.Version(1, 0, 0) } }
interfaces.notes: Locked from day one so the first real schema change is non-destructive.

interfaces.name: FaveOffMigrationPlan
interfaces.signature: enum FaveOffMigrationPlan: SchemaMigrationPlan { static var schemas: [any VersionedSchema.Type] { [FaveOffSchemaV1.self] }; static var stages: [MigrationStage] { [] } }

interfaces.name: ComparisonRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol ComparisonRepository { func record(winnerID: UUID, loserID: UUID, mediaType: MediaType, wasSkipped: Bool, flowID: UUID) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Kept as its own focused protocol, not folded into TitleRepository.

interfaces.name: InsertionRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol InsertionRepository { func pendingContext() async throws -> InsertionContext?; func save(_ context: InsertionContext) async throws; func clear(flowID: UUID) async throws }
interfaces.notes: pendingContext() drives the Library resume banner at launch.

interfaces.name: RepositoryError
interfaces.signature: enum RepositoryError: Error { case notFound; case notEntitled(PremiumFeature); case saveFailed(underlying: Error); case interrupted }
interfaces.notes: Merged: notEntitled case folded in from Codex's round-2 addition for feature-gated write attempts.

interfaces.name: InsertionState
interfaces.signature: struct InsertionState: Equatable, Codable, Sendable { var targetID: UUID; var lo: Int; var hi: Int; var lastComparedID: UUID?; var skipBudget: Int }
interfaces.notes: Pure in-memory mirror of InsertionContext; ID-based for the same drift reasons.

interfaces.name: ComparisonPair
interfaces.signature: struct ComparisonPair: Equatable, Sendable { var candidateID: UUID; var midIndex: Int }

interfaces.name: InsertionStepResult
interfaces.signature: enum InsertionStepResult: Equatable, Sendable { case continueComparing(InsertionState); case placed(index: Int, placement: PlacementKind) }
interfaces.notes: FINAL shape both sides converged on in round 2, replacing Codex's original RankingTransition/SkipFallbackReason — placement and cancellation are not representable on the same type.

interfaces.name: PlacementKind
interfaces.signature: enum PlacementKind: Equatable, Sendable { case exact(aboveID: UUID?, belowID: UUID?); case fallbackBottom; case coldStart }
interfaces.notes: Drives Placed screen's honest copy variant; never represents an interruption — see InterruptionReason.

interfaces.name: InsertionCoordinator
interfaces.signature: @MainActor final class InsertionCoordinator { func beginInsertion(for titleID: UUID, mediaType: MediaType) async throws -> ActiveInsertionFlow; func resumePending() async throws -> ActiveInsertionFlow?; func choose(_ chosenID: UUID, in flow: ActiveInsertionFlow) async throws -> ActiveInsertionFlow; func skip(in flow: ActiveInsertionFlow) async throws -> ActiveInsertionFlow; func cancel(flow: ActiveInsertionFlow, reason: InterruptionReason) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Persists InsertionContext after every step; re-fetches both compared titles by persistent ID immediately before choose()/skip() act.

interfaces.name: InterruptionReason
interfaces.signature: enum InterruptionReason: String, Sendable { case titleDeleted, userCancelled }
interfaces.notes: Fully separate from PlacementKind by design.

interfaces.name: MetadataLookupService
interfaces.signature: protocol MetadataLookupService { func lookup(title: String, mediaType: MediaType, year: Int?) async -> MetadataResult }
interfaces.notes: Never throws; offline stub always succeeds, remote lookup is additive-only enrichment.

interfaces.name: MetadataResult
interfaces.signature: struct MetadataResult: Sendable { var posterColorSeed: String; var posterInitials: String; var resolvedYear: Int?; var resolvedGenre: Genre? }

interfaces.name: OfflineMetadataProvider
interfaces.signature: struct OfflineMetadataProvider: MetadataLookupService { func lookup(title: String, mediaType: MediaType, year: Int?) async -> MetadataResult }
interfaces.notes: Deterministic hash-of-title-string, synchronous, no network — default happy-path implementation.

interfaces.name: PurchaseState
interfaces.signature: enum PurchaseState: Equatable, Sendable { case idle, loadingProducts, purchasePending, purchaseFailed(String), restored, entitled, notEntitled }

interfaces.name: ProductID
interfaces.signature: enum ProductID: String, CaseIterable, Sendable { case monthly = "com.faveoff.plus.monthly"; case yearly = "com.faveoff.plus.yearly" }
interfaces.notes: Must match the local .storekit config exactly.

interfaces.name: PremiumFeature
interfaces.signature: enum PremiumFeature: Sendable { case tvRanking, unlimitedMovieRanking }

interfaces.name: PurchaseProduct
interfaces.signature: struct PurchaseProduct: Identifiable, Sendable { var id: ProductID; var displayPrice: String; var period: String }

interfaces.name: ContrastValidator
interfaces.signature: enum ContrastValidator { static func contrastRatio(_ fg: Color, _ bg: Color) -> Double; static func meetsAA(fg: Color, bg: Color, isLargeText: Bool) -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Real WCAG relative-luminance formula, asserted numerically in tests against every locked token pair.

interfaces.name: AppEnvironment
interfaces.signature: @MainActor final class AppEnvironment { let titleRepository: TitleRepository; let comparisonRepository: ComparisonRepository; let insertionRepository: InsertionRepository; let insertionCoordinator: InsertionCoordinator; let metadataService: MetadataLookupService; let purchaseService: PurchaseService; let tasteCardRenderer: TasteCardRenderer }
interfaces.notes: Composition root built once in FaveOffApp.init, injected via .environment.

interfaces.name: ActiveInsertionFlow
interfaces.signature: struct ActiveInsertionFlow: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { var id: UUID { flowID }; var flowID: UUID; var targetTitleID: UUID; var mediaType: MediaType; var state: InsertionState; var stage: InsertionStage }
interfaces.notes: The single value driving Library's one .fullScreenCover(item:); a fresh add and a resume-banner tap construct it identically.

interfaces.name: InsertionStage
interfaces.signature: enum InsertionStage: Equatable, Sendable { case comparing(ComparisonPair); case placed(PlacementKind) }
interfaces.notes: Internal sub-state swapped inside the one shared cover; never a second cover.

interfaces.name: SheetKind
interfaces.signature: enum SheetKind: Identifiable { case addTitle, tasteCard, paywall(PaywallTrigger) }
interfaces.notes: Library owns exactly one activeSheet of this type.

interfaces.name: PaywallTrigger
interfaces.signature: enum PaywallTrigger: Sendable { case tvLock, capReached, toolbarUpgrade }
interfaces.notes: Same PaywallView instance regardless of entry point.

interfaces.name: FilterState
interfaces.signature: struct FilterState: Equatable, Sendable { var genre: Genre?; var decade: Int? }

interfaces.name: AddTitleViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class AddTitleViewModel { var name: String; var mediaType: MediaType; var year: String; var genre: Genre?; var saveState: SaveState; func save() async -> ActiveInsertionFlow? }
interfaces.notes: Failed save preserves typed content, never clears the form.

interfaces.name: SaveState
interfaces.signature: enum SaveState: Equatable, Sendable { case idle, saving, saved, failed(String) }

interfaces.name: InsertionFlowViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class InsertionFlowViewModel { var flow: ActiveInsertionFlow; var isTransitioning: Bool; func choose(_ id: UUID) async; func skip() async; func keepGoing() }
interfaces.notes: Backs the single Compare+Placed cover content view. isTransitioning folded in from Codex round 2 to gate the internal wipe animation.

interfaces.name: TasteCardViewModel
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class TasteCardViewModel { var renderState: TasteCardRenderState; func prepare(track: MediaType) async; func share() }

interfaces.name: TasteCardRenderState
interfaces.signature: enum TasteCardRenderState: Equatable { case resolvingArt, ready(Image), renderFailed(String), sharing }
interfaces.notes: renderFailed carries a reason string per Codex round 2's refinement.

interfaces.name: ToastCenter
interfaces.signature: @Observable @MainActor final class ToastCenter { var current: ToastMessage?; func show(_ text: String) }
interfaces.notes: Carries 'Title removed, comparison canceled' across the dismiss-back-to-Library boundary.

interfaces.name: ToastMessage
interfaces.signature: struct ToastMessage: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { var id: UUID; var text: String }

interfaces.name: SwiftDataContainerFactory
interfaces.signature: protocol SwiftDataContainerFactory { func makeContainer() throws -> ModelContainer }
interfaces.notes: Injectable seam; real app uses on-disk store, tests/previews use isStoredInMemoryOnly.

interfaces.name: AccessibilityIdentifiers
interfaces.signature: enum AccessibilityIdentifiers { case libraryList, comparePosterLeft, comparePosterRight, compareSkip, placedKeepGoing, addSaveButton, paywallPurchase, toastBanner }
interfaces.notes: Stable IDs for XCUITest targeting the core flow, folded in from Codex round 2.

interfaces.name: buildPrivacyManifest
interfaces.signature: func buildPrivacyManifest() -> [String: Any]
interfaces.notes: CI-checked helper keeping the required PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy fields explicit and stable; must be in the project from the first build, not added before archiving.

interfaces.name: createSampleTitles
interfaces.signature: func createSampleTitles(_ names: [String], mediaType: MediaType) -> [(String, UUID)]
interfaces.notes: Optional fixture helper for deterministic UI/flow tests, folded in from Codex round 2.
